basics.name: Hashan Fernando
basics.label: Data Scientist & Researcher
basics.summary: Data Scientist and Researcher with 6+ years of experience applying Bayesian modeling, machine learning, and NLP to healthcare and public health problems. Proficient in R, Python, and high-performance computing environments. Experienced in extracting real-world clinical data, building reproducible pipelines, and translating technical research into practical solutions. Strong background in applied statistics, deep learning, and large language models.

work.name: Advanced Computing in Health Sciences Section, Oak Ridge National Laboratory
highlights: Extracted, cleaned, and integrated VA electronic health record and mortality data, mapping ICD diagnosis and cause-of-death codes to standardized risk categories to build research-ready datasets for county-level opioid overdose modeling.

Designed a novel algorithm integrating topological data analysis and Bayesian spatial modeling to identify complex spatial patterns in opioid overdose risk across the USA, reducing uncertainty in baseline Bayesian spatial model estimates.
Built custom multi-channel CNN architectures in PyTorch to classify regional health risk strata across the USA using high-dimensional topological persistence images.

work.name: Department of Statistics and Computer Science, University of Peradeniya
highlights: Developed a smart seal and audio detection system for protecting steel cargo from tampering, leveraging IoT sensors for real-time monitoring.

work.name: Freelance
highlights: Designed and analyzed marketing performance and sales dashboards (React, Google Data Studio) pulling multi-channel spend data via Supermetrics, Shopify, and the Facebook Ads API to identify trends and inform client optimization decisions.
Managed client relationships and reporting cycles independently.

work.name: VizuaMatix
highlights: Engineered a machine learning model to identify non-performing customers in a banking system.
Developed a route optimization pipeline for food distribution trucks to improve efficiency and reduce operational costs.

work.name: MAS Holdings
highlights: Developed multiple VBA applications for planning inventory.

education.institution: Haslam College of Business, University of Tennessee, Knoxville
Postgraduate Institute of Science, University of Peradeniya
Faculty of Science, University of Peradeniya

awards.title: Merit Award for M.S. in Data Science
Half Colors
awards.awarder: Colors Awarding Ceremony, University of Peradeniya, Sri Lanka

publications.name: Unmeasured Spatial Risk in Disease Modeling: A Topological Data Analysis Approach
Identifying spatiotemporal patterns in opioid vulnerability: investigating the links between disability, prescription opioids and opioid-related mortality
publications.summary: A. Deas, H. Fernando, A. Heidi, A. Kapadia, J. Trafton, A. Spannaus, V. Maroulas.

publications.name: A Geospatial Study of Opioid Overdose Risk in Diverse Regions
publications.publisher: Conference Proceedings for International Population Data Linkage Conference, vol. 9, no. 5
publications.summary: H. Fernando, A. Spannaus, A. Kapadia.

projects.name: 1st Place Winner — MOSSAIC Hackathon (ORNL, National Cancer Institute & Leidos Biomedical)
projects.summary: Co-led a research team and empirically demonstrated data privacy leakage in cross-silo Federated Learning architectures by deploying gradient-based adversarial attacks against text transformer models.

projects.name: Clinical Document Classification & NLP Clustering (COSC-526: Data Mining & Analytics)
projects.summary: Developed an end-to-end NLP pipeline on unstructured clinical notes; generated contextual embeddings for supervised classification and applied NER, Mapper-based TDA clustering, cosine similarity, and t-SNE/UMAP for medical terminology extraction and visualization.

projects.name: Spatial-TDA
projects.summary: Developed an open-source Python package designed for extracting topological information from spatial data.

projects.name: Causal Inference for Marketing Analytics (BZAN-593: Independent Study)
projects.summary: Developed a Bayesian marketing mix model to estimate causal ROI of marketing spend from observational time-series data.
